evidence: TASK-059 verified end-to-end; closes FGAP-088. The context-install OpDefinition reflects the existing installContext engine with no behavior fork: run(cwd, params) calls installContext(cwd, { overwrite: params.update === true }) and returns { json: InstallResult } (or the error string) — the same engine and same options derivation as the /context install slash handler (index.ts:3103, overwrite from --update). surface use, authGated true (matches the gated lifecycle siblings). installContext removed from INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS; parity-check exit 0 classifying it op-backed-direct via the op run. context-install slotted into the CLI Substrate lifecycle help group; the auth-gate canonical-membership pin grown 19 -> 20 (the op auto-joins AUTH_REQUIRED_TOOLS from authGated). Runtime demo on a fresh /tmp substrate (never the live .context): context-init -> context-accept-all -> pi-context context-install --cwd /tmp/t59 --yes materialized all 16 installed_schemas[] (<dir>/schemas/<name>.schema.json) + all 16 installed_blocks[] (<dir>/<name>.json) and recorded 16 config.installed_from.assets baseline entries; a subsequent append-block-item --block requirements (REQ-001) against the bootstrapped substrate succeeded — the FGAP-088 repro (init+accept-all then unmaterialized) is resolved, the CLI bootstrap triad is complete. Gates: build + check + full test fail 0; parity-check exit 0; both SKILLs regenerated carry the op. Fresh adversarial probe PASS, 8 vectors zero defects; the verbatim-delta check found the update param faithful to the engine's overwrite + the slash --update (no invented qualifier).

criteria_results.criterion: pi-context context-install appears in `pi-context --help` under Substrate lifecycle, and groupForOp does not throw for it.
criteria_results.evidence: context-install added to the Substrate lifecycle oneOf in cli.ts; the groupForOp exhaustive/disjoint drift-guard passes

criteria_results.criterion: After context-init + context-accept-all, pi-context context-install --cwd <dir> --yes creates <dir>/schemas/<name>.schema.json for every config.installed_schemas[] entry AND <dir>/<name>.json for every config.installed_blocks[] entry (each file exists).
criteria_results.evidence: the /tmp/t59 demo: 16 schema files + 16 block files materialized on disk (ls confirmed)

criteria_results.criterion: After it, config.installed_from.assets is populated for every installed schema, byte-equivalent to what the /context install slash command produces (same installContext engine, no behavior fork).
criteria_results.evidence: 16 content_hash baseline entries in /tmp/t59 config.json; the op test asserts the op result deep-equals a direct installContext call (no fork)

criteria_results.criterion: A pure-CLI context-init -> context-accept-all -> context-install lets a subsequent append-block-item succeed.
criteria_results.evidence: append-block-item --block requirements --cwd /tmp/t59 appended REQ-001 successfully against the bootstrapped substrate
